product_overview: CanvasRead is a token-efficient AI scraping pipeline that extracts structured JSON from WebGL / Three.js canvas pages by doing in-browser pixel-diff change detection, selective crops, and constrained vision prompts. The PRD includes a three-layer pipeline diagram (see page 3) and a technology table (see page 4) that explain the architecture and chosen stack.
core_goals: Extract structured scene data from Three.js and WebGL-rendered sites (canvas-only DOM) into a fixed JSON schema.
Minimise vision model token usage to under 5,000 tokens per page by performing pixel-diff change detection and selective cropping.
Provide an interactive Next.js dashboard with live token counter, progress logs, and downloadable JSON output.
Ship an MVP with Playwright-based change detection, bounding-box crop + vision snap, 3 demo sites, and an automated TestSprite test suite.
Fail loudly and clearly for edge cases (invalid URL, no meaningful changes, cross-origin canvas) and provide documented fallbacks.

features.name: Website Scraper UI
features.description: Next.js dashboard (entry route: /) where users submit a URL, choose an interaction mode, start the scrape, watch live progress and token counter, and download the structured JSON extraction.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter website URL in input field -> Select interaction mode (static/scroll-driven/hover-driven) -> Click "Start Scrape" button -> View real-time progress logs and live token counter -> See semantic scene summary preview -> Click "Download JSON" -> Receive JSON file
Navigate to / -> Enter malformed or unsupported URL in input field -> Click "Start Scrape" button -> See validation error message (invalid URL) -> Correct URL or Cancel

Navigate to / -> Enter valid Three.js URL -> Select interaction mode -> Click "Start Scrape" button -> View real-time progress logs showing "no meaningful changes detected" -> UI shows fallback notice (will take full-page snaps every 5s after 10s) -> Pipeline finishes with warning and presents partial or empty JSON and retry option

features.name: Change Detection Engine (Layer 1)
features.description: Playwright-attached canvas observer computes a fast perceptual/block hash each animation frame, emits 'meaningful change' events only when hash-distance threshold and debounce settle conditions are met, and enforces a cap on snaps per page (default 12). See the three-layer pipeline diagram on page 3 for context.
user_flows: Playwright loads target URL -> Attach canvas observer to page canvas -> Compute perceptual hash each animation frame -> Compare hash to previous snapshot -> If hash distance > configured threshold and 300ms debounce settled and snaps < cap -> Emit meaningful change event -> Compute changed-pixel diff mask -> Compute bounding box of changed pixels -> Pass bounding box to selective snap layer

Playwright loads target URL -> Attach canvas observer -> Compute perceptual hash each frame -> No hash distance exceeds threshold within timeout -> After 10s with no meaningful change -> Trigger fallback: schedule full-page snap every 5s -> Notify UI that fallback is active -> Continue until cap or user cancels

Playwright loads target URL -> Attempt to read canvas buffer -> Playwright cannot access cross-origin canvas buffer -> Fallback to screenshot API -> Compute perceptual hash on screenshot frames -> Continue change detection or notify UI if inaccurate

features.name: Selective Vision Snap & Structured JSON Output (Layers 2 & 3)
features.description: On each change event the pipeline crops the changed bounding box, sends the crop to the vision model with a strict JSON-only prompt, receives a JSON response of labels/values/coordinates, merges into a page-level extraction document, and publishes updates to the UI in real time.
user_flows: Receive bounding box from change detection -> Crop region from full canvas -> Send crop + structured JSON-only prompt to Claude Vision -> Receive JSON-only response { labels, values, descriptions, coordinates } -> Validate JSON shape -> Merge into running page-level extraction document -> Update UI extraction preview and token counter

Receive bounding box from change detection -> Crop region -> Send crop + prompt to vision model -> Vision returns non-JSON or malformed response -> Retry structured prompt up to X times -> If still invalid, log error, mark snap as failed in UI, and continue with subsequent snaps

Receive bounding box -> Crop region -> Send crop to vision -> Vision returns clearly incorrect decorative labels -> Pipeline applies prompt few-shot examples and a confidence check -> If confidence low, flag item for manual review and continue merging other high-confidence results

features.name: Demo Mode & Preloaded Sites
features.description: One-click demo experience on the UI that loads one of three curated public Three.js sites, runs the pipeline with a visible token counter and extraction output to demonstrate efficiency to judges.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click demo site tile (demo 1 of 3) -> Dashboard pre-populates demo URL and interaction mode -> Click "Start Scrape" button -> Pipeline runs showing snaps taken and live token counter -> See final structured JSON output and token total
Navigate to / -> Click demo site tile -> Demo site fails to load or 3rd-party resource blocked -> UI shows demo load error -> Offer option to try next demo or view pre-recorded demo output

features.name: TestSprite QA Integration
features.description: Automated TestSprite test suite runs against core flows (valid scrape pipeline and edge-case scenarios) and surfaces pass/fail logs for rapid diagnosis. Test suite coverage and results are visible in the pipeline/test UI.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open Test / QA panel -> Trigger TestSprite test run for core flows -> Tests run (scrape pipeline: valid URL -> structured JSON out; token counter increments) -> See pass/fail summary and detailed logs -> Investigate failing test via log links

Navigate to / -> Open Test / QA panel -> Trigger TestSprite run for edge cases (invalid URL, no meaningful state changes, oversized canvas) -> A test fails (e.g., oversized canvas extraction times out) -> TestSprite reports failure with diagnostic trace and recommended mitigation (fallback to full-page snap) -> Developer iterates and re-runs tests

features.name: Website Scraper
features.description: Scrapes 3D or 2D websites to extract semantic scene context.
user_interactions: Enter website URL in input field
